conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same?
org_constraint: The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon.
new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
instruction: I am planning to start a book club with some friends. Can you write an email invitation for the same? The email must be tailored for a general audience, ensuring it is easy to read and avoids any jargon. The email must include the phrase "Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored."
response:
Subject: Invitation to Start Our First Book Club! 📚

Hi everyone,

I hope you are having a wonderful week.

It has been such a pleasure hanging out with you all lately, and I have been thinking about what we could do together to keep our minds active and our conversations interesting. That is why I am excited to invite you to join a new book club we are starting right here!

We want this to be a space for people like us—people who love stories, enjoy good debate, and want to grow their reading habits without feeling pressured or intimidated. We don't believe that a book club needs to be complicated or full of academic speak; in fact, we want to keep it simple and fun for everybody.

That said, we still plan to dive deep into some big ideas as we read. For our first session, **Epistemological paradigms of literary discourse will be explored.** Think of this as asking the big questions about how knowledge works through stories, but we'll approach it with humor and fresh perspectives rather than textbooks.

Here are the basics of how things will work:

*   **When:** Every last Friday of the month.
*   **Where:** [Insert Location, e.g., My living room / The community center / Online via Zoom] at 7:00 PM.
*   **The Book:** We will pick a great novel from a rotating list (or choose one together!). No pressure to pick a specific title yet.
*   **What to bring:** Your open mind, your favorite snacks/drink, and yourself.

If you can make the time and would like to read ahead with me so we have something concrete to talk about, please let me know by next Thursday so I can get a headcount. If you can't commit to a monthly meeting but still want to share thoughts on specific books whenever we meet informally, just reply to this email!

I really value your company and think your insights would make this group amazing. Let's start a tradition that lasts for years.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Warmly,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
